author: Afam Eghosa
review_text:
My stay was really enjoyable from start to finish. The atmosphere was calm, classy, and welcoming the moment I arrived, and the staff were friendly, helpful, and respectful throughout my stay.

Check-in was smooth and stress-free, and I immediately noticed how clean and beautiful the hotel looked. My room was spacious, neat, and very comfortable. The bed was soft, the lighting felt warm and relaxing, and everything in the room was arranged thoughtfully. Housekeeping also did a great job keeping the room fresh and spotless every day.

author: judith jolayemi
review_text: It is always a great experience here. Food was great. Everything was good-the ambience- the views and all. Im giving 3-star for their service because there was one of their managers who was so rash and rude to us during the buffet. Not such a good front.

author: Don Miller
review_text: Very nice.  And whe. You compare it to outside the compound...almost heaven.   But armed guards use mirrors to check the cab for bombs and your luggage is xrayed and you pass through a metal detector.   If they're trying to make me feel safe,  it ain't working...

author: Temitope Ifegbesan
review_text:
Beautiful place. I went there for an event and we used one of the lovely conference rooms.
I was impressed by the information session the hotel staff gave us before we started our event, including showing us the exits in case there was an emergency, etc.
The food too was incredible. It was a buffet and it was topnotch.

author: Olu O
review_text:
It’s a rather beautiful property; quite busy and popular too in the Ikeja, Lagos environs.
The lobby cum reception was bustling with guests checking in and out .
Went to the Grill restaurant out back by the pool area. It’s casual dining.
The service was excellent with Patrick, food was okay. My steak was cooked perfectly to the temperature I liked but lacked a bit of seasoning so was quite bland unless when eaten with the black peppercorn sauce.
The Cobb Salad… hmmm - perfection. I’ll go again just to have that.
Will review when I do stay at the hotel. For now, I only dined in.
Overall, experience was good - there was some live music that evening. I’m told it happens Wednesdays- Fridays.

author: Kenny
review_text:
My sister made a reservation for me yesterday, but unfortunately I was unable to make it as I was feeling under the weather. She decided to still go ahead for a day staycation on her own, and while trying to request an earlier check in time, the reservation was mistakenly cancelled instead of modified, which resulted in a cancellation fee due to the miscommunication.

The Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja has long been one of my sister’s favorite places not only to frequent, but also to recommend to guests who patronize her restaurant, City Diner, a successful fine dining restaurant in Maryland Mende conveniently located less than ten minutes from the hotel. Given her diverse yet very elite international clientele, the hotel consistently remains at the top of her recommendations, as the level of service, caliber of guests, and overall experience closely aligns with what her own guests expect and appreciate.

When I later called to speak on her behalf, the level of professionalism and support we received was truly exceptional. From the General Manager, Mr. Femi Bakare, to his reservations team, Olamide, who was incredibly kind and empathetic, as well as Teju, everyone went above and beyond to resolve the situation with care and understanding.

In my opinion, this speaks volumes about Mr. Bakare’s leadership and his forward facing approach to customer service, which clearly reflects throughout his team. The hotel was already one of our favorites, but the professionalism displayed by this outstanding team, combined with its proximity to the international airport, places the Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja firmly at the top of the list of the best hotels in Lagos, especially on the mainland. It is a hotel I will always choose to stay at and confidently recommend. Bravo.

author: Temi Olaniyan
review_text:
I stayed at the Marriott at the end of 2025 for about five nights, using a combination of points and cash (cash for 3 nights) at a rate exceeding $300 per night. The hotel was clean, well-maintained, and the amenities were good overall.
However, the breakfast experience was deeply concerning. Due to a demanding schedule, I did not have the opportunity to eat breakfast during the first three days of my stay. On the fourth morning—my very first attempt to have breakfast—I was turned away and informed that I was not eligible because part of my stay was paid with points.
At this price point, being denied a basic amenity felt unacceptable, regardless of policy. Around the world, loyalty programs are designed to reward guests for redeeming points—not to diminish their experience. While a manager later invited me to return for breakfast, the damage had already been done. It did not feel welcoming, and it did not reflect the standard of hospitality one expects from a global brand.
Breakfast is a fundamental part of the guest experience. Loyalty should be valued, not treated as a limitation. I share this as firm but constructive feedback—Marriott can and should do better

author: Fantasia Alexander
review_text: My boyfriend and I stayed for 1 week in March to celebrate our anniversary. A smiling Raphael greeted us at the front desk before we even reached the desk. His supervisor, Catherine, then joined him to check us in. There were no rooms ready upon arrival, but we only waited 40 minutes before getting into our room since we arrived at 10 am. The bellman, whose name I did not get, helped us in our room and was honest by splitting the tip and taking it down to Raphael as I asked. We booked a king standard room, and it was the best room that I have stayed in so far in Lagos. The modern decor and cleanliness of the room were a game-changer in Lagos. They offer both US/UK plugs, wifi and streaming services. Along with daily housekeeping, which was great as well. The food & beverage team everywhere was amazing. We were able to try every resturant but the highlight of them all was Koriko breakfast buffet. David was often my server and made sure to help me as I am disabled. Peace and Faith was also a joy to speak with as well. Peter, the food and beverage manager, also went above and beyond for me at the poolside grill Azure. I especially enjoy the 24/7 room service and cafe in the lobby. The ham and cheese croissant and coffee were my go-to. We were able to walk in at the spa and have a couple's facial for both of us. Ebere and Faith did our facials which left us feeling refreshed. I can say the chemicals were too strong for my face and it burn but healed in two days. I would recommend sensitive products for guests. On my last day, I had my first issue on my last day as I got a rude call from the front desk around 530pm about my bill, which I already settled that morning. I did not remember her name, but she was a supervisor with a bob haircut. Also, the reservation Mr. Peter set up for us was not accomedated and the service was not up to par from the day staff. They took 40 minutes to greet us and never came back to check on us after our food arrived. But overall, the previous days outweighed the last day. The staff will greet you whenever they see you and help you out whenever they see me. A big shout out to Ms. Catherine and Rapheal who spoke to me whenever they saw me. Another shoutout to Mr.Peter, who checked on me through my last day and post-stay as well. I will be returning in a few weeks, and I cannot wait to come back for another week or two.

author: ServiceCritic
review_text:
Lunch buffet was good, though the spread wasn’t so much. Waiters where out of site in the restaurant, and used plates where littered on my table, I had to call someone’s attention to it before it was clear. It should be so.

The restaurant staff cluster and gist loudly,thereby disturbing the peace and quiet of customers.

author: Scarlet
review_text:
An amazing, warm, and welcoming hotel right in the heart of Ikeja ✨
Their restaurants deliver a premium experience every time — perfect for breakfast, lunch, or dinner dates 💕 The customer service is top-notch, and the atmosphere is both beautiful and inviting, with a designated area for smokers.
The poolside is a whole vibe on its own, offering a stunning view both during the day and at night. Definitely a place worth experiencing.

author: Denisa Ryskova
review_text:
We loved staying at Marriott hotel in Ikeja. The hotel has a stylish and elegant features and very relaxing and peaceful atmosphere. All hotel staff were polite and helpful. It was our pleasure to meet one of the managers, Mr. Taiwo and also one of the engineering director. They all made our stay very enjoyable and exceptional. The pool area was very clean, and we loved spending some time in Azure and in the main lounge. The room was very beautiful and it had everything we needed for our stay. The food was delicious, made as a buffet and lots of food to choose from. I am a coffee lover and honestly, their coffee was so rich and full of flavour.

Thank you for everything.
Love from Denisa and Larry.

author: Dola A
review_text: Poor service at the rooftop buffet. I went to have a meal with family and friends at Marriott’s hotel(rooftop buffet), this was by recommendation however I was completely disappointed. A few of their staff were friendly mainly the waiters, we stood by the suya spot and the chef did not even acknowledge us or ask us what we wanted to eat. I brought this to his attention and he was rude. I would definitely not recommend this place to anybody.

author: Oluwatoyin Enisan
review_text:
The Lagos Marriott Hotel is an EXQUISITE OPULENCE and SENSIBLE Luxurious 5-star Conference Hotel.
Located in the very secure and serene environment of Ikeja GRA. A few minutes' drive will take you to the Murtala Muhammed International Airport or the domestic Airport and the Ikeja main commercial nerve center of Lagos State Nigeria.
Equipped with great parking spots, serving local and international cuisines, free Wi-Fi, lavish bedding facilities, Spa, well-equipped gym, outdoor pool and a vintage city view is your choice Hotel of all time.

author: Funmi suleman
review_text:
I had a workshop in here for 3 days and it was such a beautiful time. Ambiance, service, people on point. Very beautiful, welcoming staff and on the whole look forward to returning soon.

I returned for a spa day at Isade Spa and had a great time top to toe great treatment.

Again I returned for a dinner party of 8 at Wakame Asian Kitchen and had a tremendous time. The food was top notch Asian Fusion (Indian, Chinese, Korean) and you can mix your meals. Highly recommended.

author: Henryk Fridman
review_text: I stayed in this hotel during my layover with the crew. It's a quite busy place with various restaurants, spa, gym and pool. The rooms are spacious and clean. Bed is very comfortable. The staff is very friendly and professional.

author: Friday Akpumnonu
review_text:
This place has a grand style and good interior design, the parking space is exquisite, designed underground and very spacious, their food is glorious, the rooms topnotch and in a very conducive environment. The security too their is good and their staffs are generally nice.
It a place I did recommend for a high end luxury experience if you find yourself in Ikeja, Lagos.

author: Eric Taseera Lwanga
review_text: As a Marriott Bonvoy member, I absolutely loved my stay at this property. The service is exceptional, from Matthew at the concierge to Uzo and Elizabeth, the entire team goes above and beyond to make you feel at home with their warmth and genuine hospitality. The restaurants serve delicious food, complemented by attentive and friendly staff. A truly happy guest here! I’ll definitely be returning!

author: AshaFire Ologo
review_text:
Marriott is an Hotel with Class.. Very Neat and beautiful environment. From parking, to the Lounge to the restaurant to the Pool and to the Rooms, they are all well Placed and planned.
I will come here again and again for sure. And recommend here for people around and outside Lagos.

I will suggest more animals in the Sunday Dinner Buffet.
I tried it ones tho and I will try again to see if it's all the same...

author: tt mom2
review_text: Nice ambiance and amenities...restaurant, bar, etc.....pillows are awful though...rooms starting to show age...shower wasn't as clean and the water was only lukewarm this time..we did the couples journey through the senses 3 hour service at the spa and it was good

author: Edobong Akpabio
review_text: I love the event halls. They are comfortable. Above all, the space for car parking is awesome 😎

author: adenike maryann
review_text: Beautiful environment, well mannered staff, and a sumptuous finger food. Your safety is their concern at Marriott Hotel Ikeja. I went for a product launch held by Sahco Sifax Travels. You earn the 4star rating.

author: Olaitan Esther
review_text:
Marriott Hotel is a chain of beautiful hotels.
This is one in particular is accessible, beautiful, elegant, classy.

Located in the heart of GRA Ikeja in Lagos.

I highly recommend if you're traveling and exploring, and your priority is quality experience.

author: Ben Benjamin
review_text:
Easy booking online, good location close to airport and secure. Clean premises with clean areas. Space is a slight challenge when compared to other Marriott sites.
This is a good place to chill and be seen at but it is rather pricy.

author: Tomiwa Adebisi
review_text: Top Place and well maintained. The only issue was the traffic to get into the building. Besides that, I highly recommend.

author: Munmun Sarkarbasu
review_text: Here food is amazing. Special hotel stuffs are well behaved and good hospitality. So i recommend to everyone go there spend a good time with good food and environment

author: Nicole Adegbusi
review_text: Today I watched the security guard harass a lady. Is it because she is a female? She was seating quietly and all of a sudden the security came to harass her. I felt terrible for the lady and it was so sad to watch this happened around 12:20 pm on May 13th 2026 and 12:40pm on May 13th 2026

author: Joshua Ananaba
review_text:
It's one of the best and it's still a 4 star which is great because Nigeria can't fathom 5 star service of any industry to be honest.

But they definitely price like 5 star though.

author: Chigozie Azikiwe
review_text: Great experience always but substandard customer service. Horner, Uzor who works at the reception  and Philemon who handles events are great at their jobs.  Overall, lovely hotel.

author: gozie nwolisa
review_text:
We recently stayed at this hotel, and it's been nothing short of outstanding. From the moment we arrived, the staff made us feel genuinely welcomed—every interaction was warm yet professional. The room was immaculate, with a perfect balance of comfort and elegance; the bed felt like a cloud after a long day of exploring.

The dining options were exceptional—each meal felt carefully crafted, and the breakfast spread alone was worth waking up early for. I especially appreciated how every little detail, from turndown service to the seamless check-in, reflected a deep commitment to guest comfort.

It's been a rare experience where luxury feels effortless rather than forced. I left feeling not just rested, but genuinely valued as a guest. Highly recommend to anyone looking for a refined, truly five-star exprience.
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you so much for your kind words.\nWe’re truly delighted to know your stay was memorable. It’s always our goal to create experiences that feel both effortless and exceptional. We look forward to welcoming you back soon!"}}

author: O Ezezika
review_text:
The food and ambience were genuinely excellent and are clear strengths of this hotel.

However, my stay was significantly affected by how a serious comfort issue was handled. I was placed in a room where the air conditioning was not working. Rather than moving me to another room immediately, repair staff were sent around 1:00 a.m., disrupting the night without fixing the problem. I was only moved to another room at approximately 4:00 a.m., after several hours of discomfort and lost sleep.

What was most disappointing is that my complaint was not taken seriously even after the incident. Losing several hours of sleep had a real impact on my wellbeing and productivity the following day.

If you are ever placed in a room where the air conditioner is not working, insist on being moved to another room right away. Delaying relocation in such situations is unacceptable and avoidable.

author: Eberth Caigua
review_text: This hotel is really amazing, room are super confortable and service is very good. The only thing can be improved is the front desk, for any reason, they always say you that you don't have reservation no matter you have it

author: Physurix
review_text: I attended a conference and the hotel was indeed in the league of world class hotels. I didn’t use the rooms so I can’t speak on that, but the parking and conference halls were well built, intentional and fit for purpose. The lunch buffet was excellent with a true assortment of dishes. The location is in a very nice place of Ikeja, but driving to find the hotel was a bit awkward. A hotel of its kind should have been very visibly situated along the highway with larger grounds for parking. In all, the feel and ambiance inside is 9/10.

author: Ciccolella
review_text: My stay there was Awesome, it's the best hotel I have ever stayed in Lagos. I also attended a seminar at there hall. Everything was very orderly and professional

author: awoleye abraham
review_text:
I went for an event where they offered buffet.  The food tastes good. For the meat, the chicken meat I ate was 65% bone.
About the two elevators from the 1st and 2nd car park up to LG and UG etc, sometimes you will stand for up to 10 minutes before it comes to your floor.

author: emma nwatu
review_text: Nigeria's leading five star hotel located in the heart of GRA Ikeja, this hotel boasts of exotic service delivery. The dishes are tasteful, services are top notch and the ambience is so cozy.

author: Ayangbesan TOluwalope
review_text:
This is more than a 7 ⭐. The security is 100%. The experience was awesome.  It is a place to be again and again if you have the money.

If you want to experience luxury lifestyle and a safe place, I recommend this place.

author: Adaora Udu St. Vincent
review_text: We went there for their buffet. I wasn't so impressed with most of the food varieties but I loved their smoked ham and their pasties were delicious. I didn't see the rooms

author: Ramat Aitsegame
review_text: I attended a networking event.It's located in a serene environment, the staff were friendly and the food was great. I also want to specifically commend the Chief Security Officer for the extra attention paid to guest safety. Their comprehensive briefing on emergency exit routes and fire safety tips was highly enlightening and deeply appreciated, clearly demonstrating the organization's strong prioritization of the safety and well-being of all guests."

author: rufai rafiat
review_text: Most of their conference staff are well trained and very helpful. Marriotts tea break option beats every other person in the GRA area of ikeja. And its very affordable too( 10000) per person for conferences is cheaper than most places

author: Akosua Kumi Gyabaah
review_text: Excellent customerservice from Felix! He made my arrival in Lagos after a long flight a wonderful experience

author: shawlabrai
review_text: I attended a one day womany conference and didn't spend the night but the hall was top quality. The quality of the chairs, restrooms, parking and safety protocols were impressive

author: Nnamdi Ibemere
review_text: Beautiful and serene... If you can afford it, it's worth every penny...

author: a j
review_text: Rooms are hot the air conditioning system does NOT work it’s been broken for WEEKS! To add to it roaches have been seen creepy crawling in the rooms and the restaurants! Don’t say I didn’t warn you! Oh and none of the staff CARES!

author: michael ojei
review_text: beautiful serene,  well behaved security guard that will make you want to come back

author: I I
review_text: Rooms are a bit smaller than expected. Breakfast had fewer options than expected and the whipped cream got finished before breakfast ended. I never got the eggs I ordered for even though I was the only person on queue. The lady at the reception of the restaurant keep haggling me to seat at a certain table even when so many tables where empty

author: Henry Humphrey
review_text: Outstanding experience! Clean rooms, excellent service, great ambiance, and very comfortable. Highly recommended.

author: alero akin-amuwa
review_text:
Good experience overall.
Sufficient parking, great ambience, fair variety of meals(taste of food can be improvedupon), and restaurant options to choose from within the hotel. Good proximity to key places. Easy to locate/access. Room rates are over the top.

author: Donald O
review_text: Nice place good ambience and professional staff. I will definitely do it again.

author: Dennis Etiwe
review_text:
Beautiful aesthetic and ambiance around the hotel. I also applaud the creative use of the space to accommodate rooms, event halls, restaurants and the basement parking.

The staff were warm, friendly and helpful. The food at Koriko was also quite good.

author: UdokaObis
review_text:
The ballroom is a nice spot for events, very spacious and atmosphere was pleasant during the duration of the event I attended.

I liked the decor and the chandelier.

I plan trying their food some other time so experience the buffet

author: Confidence Udoh
review_text: Went there for a Buffet dinner and well, it was ok. I ate to my fill, several rounds, there really is no limit. But that you have to pay extra for drinks like juice and wines, that I think is not cool, cause they can be quite expensive.

author: Ju Kyung
review_text: I’ve been to Marriott Hotel on different occasions, and all the times I’ve been there I’ve been welcomed and treated nicely. Staffs are nice, food is good, decor is top notch. Fantastic!

author: Rob Storm
review_text: Nice food. Never used their rooms though.

author: Taiwo Oladitan
review_text: Wakami asian restaurant is beautiful, fantastic food and won't tear your pocket. Most of all, the food tastes fresh and delicious. Everyone of my guest enjoyed their different meals

author: V.C. Amadi
review_text: The food was very tasty and enticing. I had lunch with colleagues. I had been here before for a conference. I liked the fact that the halls and conference rooms are on a different floor from the rooms. This way, the residents will not be disturbed by noise. The whole ambience is beautiful! I enjoyed the view of the bar upstairs, and the setup is so relaxing. Overall, I'd give this spot a 90 per cent😁.

author: ONYENEKE CHIMA
review_text: Nice environment, views and room service.... But there's no swimming pool available 😭

author: Mary Armstrong
review_text: Perfect for everything! Food in all places inside the hotel is delicious.  I love the bar area food by the pool. The outdoor entertainment is magnificent.  The group has beautiful voices. It is so relaxing. Last but not least is the spa area. Get the journey through the senses spa treatment.  I can go on and on, but most of all, the people are simply genuine.

author: emmanuel okafor
review_text: They one of best conference halls and their services are top-notch.

author: Guss Technology
review_text: So I was here for a tech event which lasted a couple of days. Our event clashed with other events and everywhere turned rowdy. I must commend the security team for a brilliant job they did especially one tall dark guy, I couldn't get his name but I think he's their coordinator or supervisor as he did an amazing job being everywhere and making sure there were no mix up. I also commend the cleaners for their prompt attention to our hall. Thank you guys. To me your security team stood out. I'll recommend your hotel. The food was okay also but there can more development. Thank you Marriott Ikeja, I can't wait to be back.
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you for your kind words and recommendation!\nWe’re glad our team could support your event, and we truly appreciate your recognition of their efforts. Your feedback is noted, and we look forward to welcoming you back to our hotel soon."}}

author: Giovanna O'Neal
review_text: It might be the nicest hotel in the area. The onsite restaurants are delicious.

author: festus
review_text:
Was here for a business dinner with about 8 ppl at the Asian themed restaurant ( forgetting the name now). Despite being first to order while the restaurant was still empty at 7pm, our main meal didn't arrive until 8.30pm. fortunately, we had ordered appetizers and ended up taking the main course to go.

It was tasty though!

author: Oluwafemi Idowu Adetimirin
review_text: I was there for ESRI West African Conference 2025. It was awesome. The environment the parking lot is underground

author: Awop Gold
review_text: Great place with lots of facility and a good environment but for traffic prown area

author: MNL
review_text: Impressed by the luxurious ambiance of the hotel, keep up the good work. The standard remains same regardless the location.

author: Nicole A
review_text: We thoroughly enjoyed our stay at this hotel and will definitely be back for our next visit to Lagos. We booked an executive suite with access to the M Club and also participated in their elite appreciation cocktail evening. Although a short stay, the staff were excellent from check-in to check-out and made the experience truly enjoyable.

author: Kingsley Os
review_text:
I just love the hotel. Thier 2 level underground parking is scary 😂
Rooms are topnotch. But too busy for my liking. Not really a quiet place

author: Adeyemi Akinlabi
review_text: The atmosphere is superb but trust me your pocket must be loaded

author: aminat saidu
review_text: Firstly, we got there by 6pm in the evening and they said the rooms were not ready i was shocked because checkin time is 2pm, i asked why they said they had a late checkout, and we arrived from a long trip, how is a room not ready at 6pm, they were even unapologetic like its their right or something… secondly the staff in the restaurant most of them do not understand what hospitality is in any form, they frown their faces and are so rude… honestly for an international hotel you guys need to do better.. all you have is a beautiful room and hotel but the customer service is very bad, no one goes to a so called 5 star hotel for beauty but for service. Teach your staff to smile more and not act like the guest are their problem. You guys need to do better i have been to hotel round the world not seen anyting like the way you guys behave.. honestly hope you improve because even other guest were saying o thats how they act in marriot always rude

author: Hassan Saleh
review_text: Hotel was clean and staff courteous. The meal at Koriko & co buffet was yummy and room was cozy

author: Adedeji Matthew
review_text: They have spacious space for meetings and other events. Clean environment and good staff orientated services.

author: Idaresit “თამარი-მეფე” Akpan
review_text:
Great ambience, nice location but food was not fresh for the 2 days that I visited.
Overrated fancy food names and too expensive for the quality.

The underground car park is VERY stuffy (a warning for anyone who is asthmatic or fears enclosed spaces).

author: Winnifred Obi
review_text: Lovely scenery. Simply a touch of class and perfection

author: Charles Umoinyang
review_text: Was at an event there and it was legit cool. The buffet was very good and service was exceptional. The staff were really nice and two were exceptional the Event guy Berkeley and the Banquet Supervisor Victor. Thanks guys!

author: Fatima muhammad
review_text: Omo the place just dey ooo so cool

author: Victoria Musa
review_text: It was overwhelming in a good way. Attending a summit in a very cool environment, no noise as the hall was underground! 😍😍 I was intrigued seeing the efforts from the event management I was paying 💯 attention during the summit. I was so grateful for such occasion. When they said everyone should go for break I didn’t know we had buffet outside, I took my time inside when I came out I was wowed again. I ate some cakes and had juice. Went back in ordered my cab 🚕 everything was so easy.

author: iesha williamson
review_text: The hotel is beautiful and has everything you need in case you don’t want to go of the property. The service on the other hand is EXTREMELY SLOW. I called down to order room service and was told the minimum wait is 45 minutes for everything, no offered to pick it up by the bar and they still quoted me the same 45 minutes. I ordered by the bar, a simple lemon drop, and that took 10 minutes. The food is authentic and amazing you just need to pack your patience.

author: W . Samari.
review_text: You dey whine, This hotel is For the Big Men that wants a coded paril but whith a 5 star Hotel

author: Charles Gbegi
review_text: I love this place like 😲 so sweet experience in Marriott hotel

author: Nkechi Okoli
review_text:
The hotel is beautiful and service is great. One of the best hotels on the mainland and it's location and closeness to the airport is a plus.
Attended a dinner event at the roof top recently. Nice ambience! Food could be better taste wise but salads was really nice and fresh. A bit dark for pictures but I understand that too much light would ruin the ambience and there was also a screen so no need for light. Would come back definitely.

author: Ademidun Ogunlesi
review_text:
Really clean room.

The staff were top notch! Was given a ‘tour’ of the room as well services that they rendered.

The food was a healthy portion and could feed 2 if you’re not one that eats a lot. They did mix up my pasta order though which made me believe the kitchen staff had no clue about what I was ordering or what they could provide in the kitchen could also have been culture difference. What I know as pasta and what they understand as pasta.

I personally felt the lobby area was “too busy” and filled with people all the time. Didn’t make me feel as safe as the hotel was portraying. The lobby area is the first place one walks into and it was always packed with people “loitering”. Although, one needs a room card to access the elevators to get to ones room. Just that initial “busyness” was what threw me off.

Every other thing otherwise, was good.

author: Tunmise
review_text:
I spent the night with a friend/family and loved the experience. First, the bathroom was a dream—I could have stayed there forever! It was well-stocked with towels, toiletries (toothbrush, toothpaste, vanity kit, etc.), a hairdryer, iron, and robes. The view from the room was amazing.

The hotel follows the usual key card system for the elevators (two per room). The room had a TV, tea/coffee station, and a water heater—perfect for a self-made breakfast. The complimentary breakfast was also fantastic—kudos to the chef!

Service was generally excellent, from the concierge to other staff. However, I had an uncomfortable moment with one lady at the reception. While waiting my turn, she abruptly told me to wait, even though I wasn’t trying to cut ahead. Maybe she meant I should take a seat and return when she was done, but her approach came off as rude.

Aside from that, everything was great. Parking is adequate, security is top-notch, and the hotel offers a pool, a library, and multiple restaurants—you might not even need to leave! It was my first time here, and I truly enjoyed my stay. Hope to visit more often!

author: YT
review_text: My stays at the Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja have really never been anything short of delightful. I have always enjoyed my experiences whenever I stayed at this hotel. The staff are always courteous and polite and go out of their way to be helpful. First was Owolabi at the front desk who went out of his way to help me sort out a snag with my reservation that somehow wasn't transmitted over from the corporate headquarters - after being knackered from a 12 hour flight. Also there was Olaniyan, who was always asking if I needed anything and was just super helpful every time. Many other guys that I didn't ask for their names. The ladies at the M Club are also very courteous and professional. The staff at this hotel get A+, in my book - top notch customer service. The only thing that I've complained about here a few times are the shower configuration. They are designed weirdly that when you shower, water will always splash outside of the shower on to the bathroom floor and you'll end up with a mini pool of water after each shower. I've stayed in 4 or 5 separate rooms and this is almost always the case - some fancy design gone wrong perhaps. Minor inconvenience for a wonderful experience though. If you're in the Ikeja GRA area, check this place out. I think you'd love it.

author: Trev Nunes
review_text:
Fantastic!
After many years and stays at the Sheraton, this is a hotel in Lagos that is living up to the rest of the brand.
Clean and modern hotel, with good location to the airport. The amenities are good and the staff went out of their way to ask for feedback in order for customer satisfaction and improvement.
We enjoyed the relaxed atmosphere at the poolside bar and restaurant -where the food was served quickly and efficiently. All guests received their food at the same time and the only things missing from the menu were the locally brewed craft beers. Thanks to Otobong for service and Chef Joel in the kitchen who introduced himself and asked if the food was to our tasting, etc.
The breakfast was slightly raided and not replenished by the time we got there in the morning - but we had the hostess Fidelia looking after us and she ensured that we had all we needed.
Travelling with two children, we always ask at the time of booking for an adjoining room. This still appears to be something that the Marriott can't do, like the Sheraton. However, a much better solution to the Saffron hotel further down the road, that doesn't have any twin rooms or adjoining rooms.
We're looking forward to stopping at this hotel on our return trip through Lagos. Something that I've rarely said in nearly a decade of travelling through the various hotels in the  city.

author: Adeola Omogbolahan
review_text:
I had the pleasure of staying at the Lagos Marriott Hotel and was thoroughly impressed by the exceptional service, amenities, and overall experience. From the moment I arrived, the friendly and welcoming staff made me feel at home. The hotel's modern and sleek design, combined with its prime location in the heart of Ikeja, Lagos, made for a convenient and comfortable stay.
Their security is highly commendable such that you're check thoroughly before gaining entrance while according you with premium respect.
The room was spacious, elegantly furnished, and boasted stunning views of the city. The bed was incredibly comfortable, ensuring a restful night's sleep. I was also impressed by the extensive breakfast buffet, which offered a wide range of delicious options to suit every taste.
The hotel's facilities, including the pool, and spa, were top-notch and well-maintained. I particularly appreciated the attentive and personalized service from the staff, who went above and beyond to ensure my stay was memorable.
Overall, I highly recommend the Lagos Marriott Hotel to anyone seeking a luxurious and unforgettable experience around Ikeja, Lagos.

author: Melissa boutros
review_text:
Wakame – Marriott Hotel Ikeja: Beautiful Space, Terrible Service

I’ve been to Wakame twice now, and both visits were deeply disappointing. We arrived around 5:45 PM and were told the restaurant opens at 6. Instead of letting us wait comfortably inside, we were made to sit at the door with only a few chairs — half our group had to stand. They opened , and even then, tables weren’t ready. We sat for another 20 minutes with no plates, no tissues — nothing. Apparently, “that’s normal” here.

The last time we came, we waited an hour and a half just to get our food. This time wasn’t much better — slow, careless, and frustrating.

At the end, I simply wanted to smoke my e-cigarette — which is allowed in most places around the world. They said no, with no clear explanation. Fine, I went outside — and then someone actually came after me to say I’m not allowed to smoke outside either.
Like seriously — are you okay? Do you want us to leave? Are you trying to kick us out?

We even spoke to Vincent, the manager, and told him clearly that we were disappointed. And his response?
“It’s okay.”
What kind of manager says that? No apology, no attempt to make it right — just complete lack of care.

A restaurant is not about fancy tables or interior design. It’s about service, and making guests feel welcomed and respected. When people stop coming back and start talking badly about your place — that’s failure.

Wakame looks good on the outside, but behind that, the service is shockingly bad. Honestly not what you’d expect from a restaurant inside the Marriott.

author: Glory Obianuka
review_text:
Well, I visited the restaurant on the first floor and I must say that for a name as big as this, they can and should do better with the taste of their meals.

My meal was their Jambalaya jollof rice which I regretted immediately after I had a taste of it. The rice tasted like it's been in the fridge for a long time. I did mention my displeasure to the waitress who said she was going to let the chef know.

On the other hand, the club sandwich my partner ordered foe was perfect 👌 you should definitely try it.

For the drinks, i had the regular chapman while my partner had a drink with coffee in it, can't remember the name but it was really good.

author: Dominic Forson
review_text:
The meals were a standout feature of the hotel. Every dish I tried was not only delicious but also thoughtfully crafted with fresh ingredients.

The hotel’s fitness amenities were another highlight. The gym was fully equipped with modern equipment, from cardio machines to free weights, and had ample space for a great workout. After a session in the gym, I enjoyed the hotel’s large swimming pool, which was pristine and provided the perfect place to relax and unwind.

I recently had the pleasure of staying at the Ikeja Marriott Hotel, Nigeria, and it was an exceptional experience from start to finish.

I also felt incredibly safe during my stay. The hotel has strong security measures in place, with 24-hour surveillance and keycard access to rooms, making it feel secure and private.

I highly recommend Marriott Hotel Ikeja, Nigeria, to anyone looking for a place with excellent service, healthy dining, great fitness facilities, and top-tier security

author: Paul Oriwoh
review_text:
An exceptional luxury and unparalleled service - my stay at Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja was truly unforgettable! From the moment I arrived, I was treated to impeccable hospitality, stunning amenities, and opulent accommodations that exceeded our every expectation.

The attention to detail was impressive, the staff was warm and attentive, and the facilities were world-class. I felt pampered, relaxed, and rejuvenated throughout our stay.

A sincere thank you to the entire team for creating a truly unforgettable experience. I look forward to returning soon and indulging in the splendor of Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja once again.

Five stars are not enough - this hotel truly deserves a sixth star for excellence!

author: Dammy Pen
review_text: The rooms have a way of making me feel like a rich woman. Like a trust fund baby😃 Whenever I get the chance to go there, I feel happy. As regards their breakfast though, they need to stop cooking local (Nigerian) rice. It makes all the effort they put into the sauce and other things useless. So they should switch to foreign rice. All that money paid and the rice tastes like sorrow? NO! Every other thing about Marriott hotel is great. I give them that one😋

author: Olufemi Okeowo
review_text:
As experienced travellers from the UK and US, we found the hotel to be overpriced for the quality and service we received. We think that with a few improvements, we would definitely increase our score.

Pros
- front desk receptionist Elizabeth is exceptional. She helped my wife and I navigate a bad situation which involved the airline losing our suitcases
- A very clean hotel. Although there were a couple of instances where janitors didn't clean up after guests and we had to move tables due to food on the floor in the lobby
- Great location
- Liked that there were multiple restaurants available for dining. Great that there was a late night menu which was amazing for us who arrived late in Nigeria after flying in from the UK
- The rooms are noise proof. We didnt hear other guests
- Overall, good customer service from hotel staff

Cons
- Food was not the best quality. My wife was sick daily after eating at the various restaurants. The steaks we ordered from the pool bar were low quality, and they got our orders mixed up
- Overall, overpriced for the service we received and stay. We had to wait a minimum of 45 minutes for any food/drink order.
- There were no premium products in the bathroom.
- The elevator system is very flawed. This view was shared by the multiple guests we shared the elevators with too. Its a functional issue which can be resolved to minimise waiting time
- The meal options were not up to a standard we expect from a Marriott hotel, and for the price we paid
- The bathroom sink was blocked. When it was fixed, the plumber did not move my wife's things and they were soaking wet. These were items which were needed as we were due to have our traditional wedding the next day. As a result, we had to stay up late cleaning and washing these items

author: Richard C.
review_text:
This hotel is very beautiful, the rooms are great, staffs are fantastic. Chris, Kennedy, Lydia, Victoria and Maureen are great staffs. Food is wonderful. There are some things that needs improvement though, the fake grass carpet on the first floor by pool is a safety hazard. Marriott has to do better on crowd control as so many people flood the lobby just to take pictures, this takes up space for residential guests to relax at the lobby.
The entrance is too small for the amount of rooms here, this is the reason for so much traffic congestion at the entrance. The stairs/ fire exit doors can only be opened from the inside, another fire hazard. The elevators needs to be manned always as people exceed the 10 persons capacity most of the time. The service is wonderful and professional.

author: Frank David
review_text: Went in for a Family dinner for 6 after about some months of my last visit. The environment still maintains its state of the art cleanliness. However, the food quality, (Different Rice Varieties tasted) and soup were not of the renowned quality. Secondly, the desert was just plain sugary all through. Lastly, the quality of staff service was below expectations. There was much pressure on the available staff on duty, as it appeared they were under staffed.

author: Angel Winter
review_text: A true luxury 5 star hotel situated in the heart of Ikeja GRA. Chilled at the Idapo lounge and the ambiance of this restaurant is truly breathtaking. From the moment you walk through the door, you are met with an air of sophistication and elegance. The lounge area is beautifully decorated, with plush seating and soft lighting. The interior design is a work of art, with intricate details and a perfect balance of modern and classic elements. The atmosphere is relaxing and inviting, creating a space where you can truly unwind and enjoy a meal. Overall, this restaurant is a true five-star experience, with everything from the decor to the service.

author: Oreoluwa Omowaye
review_text:

The rooms are a bit expensive but they’re worth every penny. Now, let’s talk about the restaurant. The restaurant serves a buffet meaning you walk up to what you want and serve it yourself. Their food is so delicious that you’d want to go back to get more.
They also have ballrooms for events and parties. The ballrooms are humongous, soundproof and technically advanced to some extent.
They’ve got excellent parking space and great customer service too. You will want for nothing in this hotel.

It was a beautiful space, the lobby is  amazing, and I felt really warm. I liked the design alot, well lighted.

The concierge, the security guys at the entrance and in the garage, the house keepers were all really cool guys, they did their job with a smile and were happy to answer my questions.

However, the receptionist were not as nice. They hardly smiled, though they did their job. I have worked as a customer rep guy before and I understand the importance of a smile.

Another thing I forgot to ask was why children were not allowed in certain lounge. I had to leave my child with a friend when I went to have dinner just before we left and I wasn't happy with that.

Anyways,I will definitely come back again, and I know the service will be better. See you soon!

author: Martins Close
review_text: Beautiful ambience with very polite staffs . They need to work more to brighten the underground car park

author: Tina Eze
review_text:
From the entrance to the underground parking space to the beautiful views and high-tech equipments. It was a delightful time and I can't even begin to express how I felt.

It almost felt like I was outside Nigeria 🇳🇬 😅
I didn't lodge though so I can't say I know how big or beautiful the rooms but I can tell you that if you see a picture of it, believe that its even more beautiful in reality.

I enjoyed the Friday happy hour and I'll definitely make it a good habit to do that more often ☺

author: Titilola Peters
review_text: It was a family visit to have lunch with a visiting uncle. We had lots of fun and the kids didn't want to leave. The hotel is classy yet homely. It's definitely a place to bring the family for a fun weekend, if you can afford it 😁. Rates are quite high!

author: victor emerson
review_text:
Went there for a breakfast meeting in the restaurant . Sat in the lounge afterwards for a coffee. Restaurant served food buffet style and there was wide variety of food spanning several continents. Service isn't the  restaurant was awesome.. Price for food value. Lounge offers an appropriate facility. For short informal meetings. Very good ambience
It can however be extremely busy and this can be distracting..

author: chibuzor ugwuja
review_text: Great ambience, the food was good too. It was a buffet lunch so it had to be good 😊

author: eyimofe onifade
review_text:
We sat at the pool lounge. The lady that attended to us was fantastic. She did a very good job.

The complaint we had about another customer was quickly  resolved.

The DJ last Friday night was 100%. He did a very good job. I would hire him if I needed a DJ 😁.

The food though 🤦. My expectation was higher 😃

I enjoyed the ambience.

author: Medinat Oyedele
review_text: I went there for a conference for 3 days and it felt like I should go back home😁. I love everything about the hotel. The staff were so helpful , welcoming and professional.  Their meal is super! I love the varieties they have on their menu. Free Wi-Fi! Beautiful environment. Great artistry and blend of contemporary art.

author: Kat Dohallow
review_text:
Wonderful customer service and very helpful staffs.  Beautiful hotel and delicious meals from room service.

SPA was amazing I bought a 3 hours package. Was so relaxing 🥰

author: DEBBY ACHIEVERS
review_text: Go with your card or you'll be disgraced. They do not accept any other form of payment. After eating a meal which was supposed to have been paid for with the group I came with I was still asked to pay separately because according to them, my group has cleared their bill. I agreed to pay but I did not have my card... I was delayed there at the restaurant, they weren't giving me any alternatives,  just keeping me there , walking back and forth from restaurant to reception... very annoying . I almost cried. I had to meet the host of our event to help settle the bill which I didn't want to bother him about initially... sad experience 😢.

author: Lateefah Muhammad
review_text:
A cosy place to spend time.
The room was neat, and the bathroom was sparkling clean.

There are no signs of mosquito or insect anywhere in the hotel. They have a lounge and bar. There are about three different restaurants as well to choose from.

If you need to order a room service, in terms of food, be sure to give about an hour or 90 minutes before the food arrives (my experience, though).

author: Dr. Fergus Enegide
review_text: Great ambience,  perfect location. The finishing is superb, and the aesthetics in the interior are unbeatable. Service personnel are well trained and willing to go extra miles to ensure customer's satisfaction. Relish the view from the room if you are on the upper floors. You don't need to be in dubai; this place is Dubai in Lagos. Try their buffet and thank me later.

author: oyinlola oyesiku
review_text: Cool ambiance, air conditioned, and classy. Since they just opened everything is bright and shiny. Had dinner there (buffet) and the staff were really pleasant from the security to the waiters. I love the decor, it’s has a wooden theme which is beautiful. Not a fan of the chair at the restaurant tho, it’s cute but the back is too low. The food was alright, only had a few things. Will definitely go back to try some other areas of the hotel. Well done Marriott.
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you for your kind words and the honest feedback.  We look forward to welcoming you back again."}}

author: Isaac O.
review_text:
Absolutely stunning! From the entry point to reception, it's all class. The facilities are state-of-the-art. I was pleasantly satisfied with the quality of service there. The staff at the parking lot were very polite. The event hall is fantastic. There's ample parking space for a good number of cars at a time.

Location is relatively accessible. There are multiple entry points and parking is free.
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you so much for your kind words. We look forward to having you back at our hotel."}}

author: Balikis Alli
review_text:
I attended a conference here just last week... it's a beautiful place and the staff have courtesy and are ready to help..
Their hall for a small gathering isn't bad at all, the restroom are very clean, but I think they should work on the female toilet, their is no water accessible in the toilet if you want to wash up, you can only use tissue, which isn’t comfortable for me and some other ladies as well. Aside that, their service is perfect.

And of course the breakfast and lunch taste so delicious and you have wide varieties of food, pastries, fruits and Juice

author: Kik Obi
review_text:
My recent stay at the Marriott Lagos exceeded my expectations in every way. From the moment I stepped into the grand lobby, I was welcomed by the warm smiles of the staff, who provided seamless check-in assistance.

Rooms:
The room was immaculately clean, spacious, and elegantly designed, with modern amenities that ensured my comfort. The bed was incredibly plush, and I appreciated the thoughtfulness of the amenities, including a coffee maker, quality toiletries, and high-speed Wi-Fi.

Dining:
The on-site dining options were outstanding. The breakfast buffet featured a mix of local Nigerian and international dishes, all prepared to perfection. The jollof rice at dinner was a standout, rivaling some of the best I’ve ever had. The attentive staff ensured every meal was an experience to savor.

Facilities:
The hotel’s facilities were world-class. I enjoyed the rooftop pool with stunning city views and the fully equipped fitness center, which helped me stay active during my trip. The spa was a serene oasis, offering rejuvenating treatments that left me feeling refreshed.

Service:
What truly set Marriott Lagos apart was the exceptional service. The staff went above and beyond to make my stay memorable, from assisting with local recommendations to promptly addressing any requests. Their professionalism and friendliness were unmatched.

Location:
The hotel’s central location made it convenient for exploring Lagos. It was close to key attractions, and the concierge provided invaluable guidance for navigating the city.

Final Thoughts:
Marriott Lagos strikes the perfect balance between luxury and comfort, making it an ideal choice for both business and leisure travelers. I highly recommend it and look forward to returning on my next visit to Lagos.

author: TheAdunni Banjo
review_text:
The ambience in this place was everything and it looks so beautiful at night. It was a friend’s birthday and she had her dinner here at the hotels restaurant where there was buffet.

For a buffet, I expected that there would be more options and proteins added but we just had a few varieties to select from but I guess it was because they were about closing . I had mashed potatoes and pork sauce and it had asparagus and other vegetables in it but honestly the food tasted bland and I just had to stuff it in cause I was really hungry at the time. Maybe it was not just my lucky day because I heard great reviews about this place but oh well…

Food aside, great place for taking quality pictures but it was a bit rowdy so you just have to be patient because people move around every minute.

author: Adetutu O'Seye
review_text: Lovely place to be. The interior finishes and colour blending is beautiful and top quality, spacious lounge. The food is great. It's just the place to be if you can afford it. You might just get carried away looking around and probably miss your appointment..😅

author: Gail Ross
review_text: Love this hotel in Ikeja.Great property with nice amenities.
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you for sharing your experience.\nWe truly appreciate your support and are glad you chose to spend time with us. We look forward to welcoming you again soon."}}

author: AKINDELE AKINSANYA
review_text: A lovely place to be right from the drop off zone to the reception. The reception looks very interesting and enticing. I like the transition from the reception to the Swimming area with segments of sit-out leaving customers with choices. A nice ambience but it can be better because some areas fell short in terms of maintenance when I visited and I think that can be improved upon. A nice experience in summary.

author: Ekene Ajiri-Uwuseba
review_text: A perfect place to stay in Ikeja. The design is perfect and parking is not a problem as they have different levels of parking. It's a place away from home with all comforts.

author: Nicolas
review_text: Lovely place to be, and I visited the Idapo lounge. Great customer service, the ambience good 👍 my evening was well spent, though I forgot my ATM and the cash on me was less, but they don't collect transfers, which was kind of  off for me all the same I enjoyed myself the food was tasty 😋 😊

author: Derah Nmah
review_text: Lovely environment. If you love pictures there are so many lovely backgrounds to use but get ready cause the security will stop you from taking pictures don't know why. The calmness is epic. They observe the covid protocol. I liked the place

author: Deborah Aghahowa
review_text: Lovely mind-blowing ambience, had lunch with colleagues at the Sunday buffet, food was good on the average, lots of variety, though the buffet area was crowded literally, but we found an outdoor spot that was nice and cool below the pool, though we were disturbed by flies. The attendants could definitely do better in terms of language and presentation, but overall it was a good experience.

author: Folayemi Abayomi
review_text:
I came here to book a space for an event.
The outdoor area is beautiful, the banquet hall is 👍, and the persons I spoke to were all courteous.
There was so much noise from the guest at the lounge/reception though. So if you're like me who just want to get around your business peacefully, you might not enjoy the time at the reception.
Generally, it's a beautiful place and I look forward to hosting my event here.

author: Birendra Kumar Singh
review_text: Had a wonderful stay at this gorgeous property in March 24. A great ambiance and cool upscale hotel located in Ikeja area  near Lagos Airport. Found very neat and clean and the room was equally good. The pool area has multiple restaurants besides Restaurants located on Ground and First floor. Asian restaurant Wakame serves Indian food which was really a plus for me. The residents were global in nature including people from USA, Germany, China and of course Africans, making it truly global. Look forward to staying here again.

author: Bronwen Hobbs
review_text: I have never been treated as badly at a hotel in 17 years as a flight attendant as I was by the front desk attendant. I’m a Bonvoy Gold member, and very well traveled, and have worked in hotels, so I am always patient and understanding with delays or issues, but the attendant was so rude and dismissive, I could not believe it.

author: oluwakayode okesola
review_text: Very beautiful hotel and lovely environment. I enjoyed every minute I spent there. The rooms are really lovely, the lobby is so beautiful and the pool side awesome. I give them 5 stars because they deserve it and I'm hoping to visit there really soon

author: Gbenga Ezekiel
review_text: Hotel in its finest. Cozy and beautiful.

author: Sarah-Jane Starnes
review_text: What’s an amazing hotel, the staff are friendly , helpful. They make you feel welcome. Joy at the front desk checks on guests to ensure everyone is happy and all needs are met. The staff at the pool Restuarant give you the best service. King the chef goes out of his way to accommodates all diet requirements. The staff in the lounge are welcoming, friendly and give a great service. Well done I had a great stay. Thanks team Ikeja Marriott you’re all great.

author: Olisa Chukwuma
review_text: Absolutely stunning. The buffet had everything and they all tasted good. Great hotel. The food selection was quite good. Compliments to the chef for the lasagna served on Wednesday. A good place for family or business trip. I hope the standards are maintained.

author: Tigerlilly Helen Blyton
review_text:
Extremely cosy environment so I'd recommend a shawl during evening events. Good food and enough wide variety of drinks and locations to enjoy within the hotel. Very much like Dubai in Lagos. Perfect for us for a night out were contemplating an anniversary event.

.

author: Afeez Dosunmu
review_text: Lagos Marriott Hotel is top-notch with a truly professional touch. I once forgot my phone at the reception and a security officer picked it up and kept it safe that alone says a lot. The gym is well equipped, the beds are super comfortable, and the overall vibe is classy and welcoming. Great experience all round! They also have good food and different restaurants for food and relaxation

author: Maduka Francis
review_text: Lagos Marriott is a 5 ⭐ hotel located at Ikeja GRA. It's a great place for business travel, holiday trips, events, etc. The atmosphere is serene in the midst of lots of guest. It's beautiful and decent!

author: Diokpa One
review_text: Can easily be classed as the best hotel in Lagos. Extremely neat, excellent service, good food, nice rooms. I mean it's still relatively new. Very polite and efficient staff. The hotel boasts of ample car park with 2 levels underground parking. Decent use of space.  A number of meeting rooms and halls to host various events. I mean I rarely give 5 stars, but on this occasion, I can't fault this hotel. I hope they can keep it up and keep the facility well maintained in the years to come.

author: Charles Keku
review_text:
Had had breakfast and lunch meetings here. I've also experienced the dinner buffet.

The breakfast was my favorite.

Can't really remember the lunch. But for some reason, I think the dinner menu didn't have sufficient variety as I expected.

Whenever I'm having a buffet, it's the variety for me.

Other things of note include ample car park, courteous staff and welcoming and comfortable environment.

author: Jennifer A
review_text:
Pros: Lovely rooms, very clean and comfy and TV options were great. Could Cast on the TV also. Internet was super fast. Room overlooked the pool so great views. Very nice meeting areas with lovely paintings and the air conditioning was excellent. Coffee maker in the meeting rooms!

Cons: Could not turn off the extractor fan in the room which was a bit distracting. The reception area was so so busy may be as checked in over the weekend. No gluten free bread or cereal for breakfast which is unusual for a Marriot. Lunch was also insufficient for the crowd that came and food contents not marked- so couldn't tell if there were any allergens.

review_text: Very lovely but expensive 😅😂

author: esther davies
review_text:
Perfect ambience, premium and luxury. (Perfect picture location)
Hotel services is top notch. (Safety & security)
Lovely restaurants, lounge,  coffee and drink bars,
The conference/board rooms are perfectly situated on the basement floor according to capacity of guests.
Sufficient underground car parks below the basement with staffs to assist your experience.

author: Michael Monwuba
review_text: We visited Nigeria for the Christmas break and decided to have buffet at the Marriott. We went there for a Sunday buffet. The food was amazing, live music was a master class but the service was poor. We were in the outside seating area and it was almost like we had to request for one thing multiple times before it was provided. I’m his definitely took away from the dining experience, hence the 3 stars. The environment was clean and beautiful.

author: Amaka Nwako
review_text: I love love this place. We came here for a conference and the serenity is beautiful 😍 Their restrooms are topnotch and there is sockets everywhere for you to power your devices. Love the ambience of this place.

author: Rosemary Okandeji
review_text:
So I had this 3-day regulatory training at Marriot.
Halls tagged Ballroom 1, 2, 3 etc I guess. It's ambience is top notch. Their are also private meeting room of 20 people capacity.
The staff are courteous and professional in their approach.
Their meal also ok. I enjoyed my visit for the three days.
You would forget your problems when you are here...lol...cos you cannot see the outside world.
I love Marriot hotel. Their display of artifacts at a Corner is also cute.
I would recommend you go try this out.

author: Kemi O
review_text:
Aesthetically pleasing and popular Hotel in GRA.
They have various spaces and areas for getting a quick drink, food, lounging or hanging out.
There aren't enough signs and directions to help visitors.
We got into an elevator trying to get to the parking lot but it kept taking us to other floors... only to find out from a staff that the elevator doesn't go to the parking lot so we can had to walk across to the other end of the hallway to use another elevator.  If only they had such information by and in the elevator.
Ordered food by the pool which took a little while to arrive.  Club sandwich was great.
BBQ burger could have tasted better.
It gets really busy on weekends so outdoor sitting maybe be limited.
They have ample parking underground and valet parking is available too!

Update I
Their dinner buffet was decent. It cost 19,500NGN
They could have done better with the variety of foods. Only fruits available were apples and pineapples.
The speed of replacing finished foods wasn't fast enough and we got better tasting and looking foods by arriving early.

The room was clean and had basic necessities.
They had some seats too close to pool and their staff kept telling us not to swim around that area.

author: Hemanshu Katira
review_text: Went there for dinner only @ wakame Asian restaurant. Nice ambiance, good food. Need improvement in service, staff menu training and stocking of whiskeys. Ordered 3 different whiskeys from their menu and none available. Had to settle for what was available.

author: Abimbola Kusanu
review_text: Nice ambient place. Just that I don't like underground parking 🅿️

author: Chidera Aniagolu
review_text: Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja offers a modern and luxurious experience, with well-designed rooms, excellent amenities, and top-tier customer service. The hotel's facilities, including its pool, fitness center, and restaurants, are highly praised, making it a popular choice for business and leisure travelers. Despite being located in the busy Ikeja area, the hotel provides a calm and comfortable retreat, though its pricing may be on the higher side for some guests.

author: Akinwale Ogundipe
review_text: Wow! This is the best I have visited in Nigeria. Personally I proud of the standard of equipments and services provided. Good rooms, controlled access to floors, clean environment, and very good food. A nice place for the family and meeting with professionals and friend. The location is good too, within 5 to 10 minutes from the International Airport. I will jump at any opportunity to visited this hotel, again, and again.

review_text:
Absolutely Loved it
The atmosphere was spectacular, the food was marvellous, the bedrooms and meeting rooms were lovely... If there was anything that detracted from the experience, it would be how slow the elevators were and the absence of stairs as an option.
I should take the time to famz the food again. They had  the best food I have had at a hotel; everything was great. author: Aisha B
review_text:
Beautiful aesthetics! very nice, ultra modern decor! Beautiful place to take pics!
I did the Buffet , Price was higher than that of other hotels of same rank and tried out most of the food. The taste was not it, in simple words, I was disappointed 😢.
Customer service was fair.
I don’t think I will be going there again

author: David A
review_text: I went out to eat here and couldn't get a spot and to make matter worse the staffs don't know what floor their Asia restaurant is located l had to spend 39min to locate the place. On getting there, l was told is a  buffet section, and all they have was empty pots and pan of food. I had to leave the building.

author: tokunbo adodo
review_text:
It's a Marriott.
And it ticks most of the boxes with regards to the expected standard particularly the ambience.
Parking is definitely not the expected standard, the drive to it is a little clumsy and the actual location can be very very hot.
Interior decoration (for the lounges) is just good enough but some elements will need to be changed soon.
Food and drinks are a hit or miss.
I didn't stay in the rooms so I can't comment on those.

author: Manuella Onigbanjo
review_text:
The ambience of the terrace ministered to me on sight. I love the open air and how spacious the seating is. The music was just right, in volume and tempo — this was late afternoon. The atmosphere really just breathed pleasantness and relaxation.

I had their lunch buffet and the food variety could be better. My friend had turkey from the grills, which appeared to be bland on the inside.

Thankfully, the manager stopped by our table and asked how the food was. We gave feedback that the turkey was bland. He apologised and had a pair of wings grilled with proper seasoning. This time, the seasoning penetrated the turkey and it was far better than the first attempt.

I love that the manager went around ensuring their customers were actually satisfied with the quality of service. I just wish the variety for the buffet were wider and more interesting.

I had such a lovely time, it didn’t occur to me to take photos. Lol.

author: OMOTAYO SAKA
review_text:
Was my first time here and I was immensely impressed. Right from the parking which is 2 stories underground and well manned and directed to the security you have to pass through to get to the elevators. Then the lobby screams downtown Houston if you get carried away.
The room I was in was well laid out, moderately furnished and simple enough.
The service was 5 stars 🌟. The people I went to see called for their bags to be picked up as they were checking out and the guy arrived in under 5 minutes. He was very pleasant, professional and friendly.
The only thing icky for me is the location,  other than that great hotel 🏨
Definitely visiting again with friends

author: Rx Mams
review_text:
Loved my stay here.
A beautiful place indeed, with wonderful services.
I enjoyed the food as well.
The only thing I didn't enjoy was the long wait for the elevator, and the elevator opening on floors that the persons have already used another lift, making the journey up or down even longer.

review_text: Great hospitable room, cosy, home away from home. Central location just 10mns from the airport, great food, lots of water in the room. Would like to visit again.

author: Oluwakemisola
review_text: Not bad at all.

author: Victory Omuera
review_text: Such a magnificent and aesthetically pleasing place, the indoor layout and design gave me an eye-full. The entrance is well-coordinated. The event halls are sound-proof, spacious and conducive for high class corporate and ceremonial events

author: Lawal Dele
review_text:
A nice structure , great parking service ( the best I’ve seen so far in Lagos ). Well designed interior and aesthetically pleasing. However find the pool bar , restaurants by the pool a tad bit to lumped up. Other than that , they have a nice array of good red wine selections, coffee is great and also service is great.

Would definitely come back to try the food .

author: Dara Femi
review_text:
The hotel is a beauty to behold. Well located in the hearths of Ikeja GRA.
If you are thinking luxury and security, this is the place to be. From the moment you enter into the lobby of the hotel, to its restaurants (I visited koriko & co)  to the pool side, it was amazing. I still feel there is more to be done about the crowd of non guests at the hotel. I can't help but narrate the experience with the staff, they are very welcoming and ready to assist guests at every point.

author: Femi A
review_text:
Service was excellent. Their breakfast was lovely too. I think it's great to have included yam in their breakfast. That's a very good cultural inclusion. Nigerian hotels should include Nigerian food on they menus.
Keep up the good work

author: Maduabuchukwu Amande
review_text: Amazing, lovely, beautiful hotel. I don't have the exact words to describe it but it's really really amazing. The rooms are so cool, the pool area is fantastic. Breakfast cost N11,000 per person and its advisable to book from the hotels website. You want to experience this, expensive and worth it.
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you so much for your kind words. We look forward to having you back at our hotel."}}

author: Kingsley Oluwaseun Uwadia
review_text:
Do you want that royal treatment like the king and queen you are?

Do you want to have a good time with your spouse, family and friends?

Do you want to host your guest from different locations or family and friends from Europe, America and other parts of the globe with good security and a safe place to stay?

Then Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja is the place to be.

From the security men at the entrance gate to the guys at the door into the beautiful well crafted structure.

To the ladies at the reception to welcome you in and the other staff at the underground car park with good signs to point you to the right place to park

The waiters, room service and different well-trained personnel

The good tasty well-cooked food and the different kinds of soda drinks, spirits and wine.

Sumptuous pastry and dessert

The rooms are beautiful with a lovely bath area. If you like me who likes to have a good view of the bathroom with my spouse, oh you're in the right place.

The in-house entertainment is mind-blowing.

The swimming pool is well taken care of and with good hands to assist you.

They've got a good pamper area for your body massage to help you relax.

Take out time to go check them out
👌

author: Orevaoghene Agba
review_text:
Absolutely gorgeous place!

The aesthetics are tremendous, the architectural works perfect. It's a really classy place with classy and courteous staff. Great ambience...its also very spacious and neat. The environment is serene, great for relaxation/meetings.

COVID-19 protocols are adhered to.
The food is really good. The restaurant offers an all you can eat menu consisting of different kinds of local and 'foreign' dishes.

author: bassey edoho
review_text:
Had great experience staying here. Just putting that out there so we are clear!

Access
Because of the location of the hotel and distance from the road, driving in can be a bit of a strain. You might find yourself on a line to drive in because of the time taken to grant access to a vehicle (which is good for security reasons). If you are being driven, you can be dropped off at the hotel's entrance but if you are driving, you'd have to get to the parking area which is beneath the building and a little cumbersome. But don't worry, service men here are quite nice and helpful. Listen carefully to directions so you don't get to levels that are restricted. If you are lodging, you'd have access, don't worry.

Ambience
I will not speak too much about this. Just look at the photos. The place is probably nicer in reality compare to most of what you'd see.

Accommodation
Comfy! Cosy! And all the synonyms you can remember. Rooms are secured and have what is required to give you comfort away from home.

Food
This is my best part as a food adventurist. I must say the range is good however, I expected better. I believe they have the potential to up the game from the level it was at the time I visited. I'd expect better plating, more options and flavour. Service, however was great.

In conclusion, generally, you won't be making a bad decision coming here. You'd love it. Trust me
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you so much for your kind words. We look forward to having you back at our hotel."}}

author: Lanre Odesilo
review_text:
I recently had the pleasure of staying again at the Lagos Marriott Hotel. I can say for sure it has provided my best experience when back home in Lagos. It definitely is shoulder high above the other four Marriott brand properties in Lagos, all of which I have had the privilege of staying in the past.

Staff are very professional & courteous. Facilities and amenities are in tip-top shape (hope this is maintained continuously). The junior suite (overlooking the pool) I was upgraded to (courtesy of my Marriott Elite status) was quite comfortable. The M-Lounge service was superb with a very good array of menu - Rita, Maureen, Gbenge and the rest of the team provided the best possible level of service.

I look forward to my next stay and will highly recommend to anyone visiting Lagos.

Please keep in up!!!

author: Choco Late
review_text:
Edit: I’m big time disappointed at the customer service this year. These people are doing more talking than listening lately. The passive aggressive tone. The lack of compassion for a customer who is going through grieve and asking a modification for a 1 night booking. I’m very disgusted to say the list to think I wanted to modify my reservation with booking without a penalty to a later date with them due to a recent loss. They are being very irrational even with me wanted to push my date. That’s greed and a show of appreciation for a returning guest. The refusal to let me speak to a manager and non chalant behavior speaks volumes! Horrible.

Marriot Ikeja is worth all the hype and more. I was here during this past Christmas period 2022 for a couple days prior relocating to the island and I enjoyed every bit of my stay. Serene environment, amenities, food, ambiance and staff were top notch.

Though there was a bit of issue with the staff  by the pool side initially profiling me as a “ random girl who came there to get pictures” and pretty much embarrassed me so much and even refused me service. Needless to say this led to my wanting to leave. However, seeing that these staff are just a couple compared to the nice people I had came across during my stay.

I reached out to the amazing staff whom had initially booked my room via phone while I was in the states (Ima) regarding my complaints and she immediately stepped in and also invited the attention of the The manager “Mr. Teklo” I believe is his name. Mr. T responded immediately to handled the issues, and did so professionally and prompt with such grace yet sternness and held their staff accountable for this unfavorable treatment towards myself and my guest. Thanks for everything! The apology wine and treats. I’ll def be returning 🙌🏾

author: Eli U
review_text: My room experienced flooding, and the bathroom lacked a door. Manager Adeola displayed extreme rudeness and unprofessionalism. However, Supervisor Henry was helpful, offering an alternative room to address the issue. Adeola falsely claimed that Marriott does not accept bank transfers due to a holiday in Nigeria, but another staff member accepted the transfer, allowing me to make payment. This dishonesty made my stay unpleasant. The hotel’s standards seem to have declined since my last visit two years ago when the staff were professional. Management should investigate and refund both the money and points spent, as this was an unsatisfactory experience. I won’t be returning.

author: S.O.U.L ARTS
review_text:
The ambiance is the best thing about this place. It's so divine and exquisite! I can only speak on the buffet as that's what took me there. The chairs are a bit uncomfortable in the restaurant and there's a lack of variety in all 3 courses. Very limited options for starters, mains and desserts. I had the Efo riro and it was way too spicy. I enjoy a spicy meal but this was too hot even for me. Same as the Peppered chicken and fish. I'd advise they tone down the pepper and whoever wants more pepper can add to their meal from the pepper holder proveded on each table.
Another inconvenience was the fact that we had to call ahead to book for a table. We did and got confirmation. However, when we got there, we were not allowed in as our names weren't on the list. This was embarrassing to say the least. However, they eventually found us a table.
In all, I'd say it's a really lovely place to visit. The staff were very helpful and polite! 👍🏽👍🏽I'm sure with time, the restaurant will get its groove. Let's just say these were kinks in a new establishment.
I will recommend this place to anyone. 👌🏽
