author: Debis Uzezi
review_text:
Lagos mariott hotel was a fancy experience and i had a lovely brunch at the koriko restaurant ... My reservation which i made a week prior to the date was so easy to make online and the site is very easy to navigate. I was checked in promptly and the ambience was a fantasy, very well lit restaurant, helpful servers with great customer service. Their chef was super friendly and on hand to guide food selection, The food was amazing from the shrimp salad to the fish jollof rice, it was really tasty. My only criticism was the food selection was too garlic forward, more options for people who are allergic to garlic and it almost posed an issue as i had only a few garlic free food options to pick from. The dessert menu needs a little improvement, i had a flan that i didn't really love the taste and the options were a bit thin. As it was a Sunday, it was packed full and for people who dnt like crowd, i suggest picking a weekday to visit. I'd also like to commend them on their covid protocols even with the crowd, they enforced the necessary protocols. I'd definitely be visiting again.
P. S their parking space is so huge and this was an added plus for me.
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you so much for your kind words. We look forward to having you back at our hotel."}}

author: DennisLagos
review_text: In a bustling mega city like Lagos that is not short of exquisite 5-star hotels, Lagos Marriot holds its own place of pride. Distinctly imposing and nestling on a prime expanse of grounds along upscale Joel Ogunnaike Street in GRA Ikeja, Lagos, Nigeria, Marriot indeed brings its international standard to bear on hospitality. My visit to the hotel on a week day was simply for a scheduled brief meeting and it was my first. Accessing its dainty, serene premises is easy, yet under eagle-eyed human-technology security checks. Guests driven from the manned gates up to the main all-glass entrance are calmy, warmly welcome by well-dressed attendants who step forward to open car doors for guests to step out. Those with luggage are helped too. Security formalities require luggage to be placed on an automated conveyance belt from the entrance inwards the hotel, while valuables like phones are also placed in a handy holder. Once guests pass through the secure revolving glass doors and emerge in the hotel, they pick up their luggage and proceed to the reception. The hotel lobby is quite spacious, neat, illuminated and well air-conditioned that guests feel immediately much comfortable and welcome. The receptionists are courteous and everyone moving around on duty or for leisure is at ease. Plush sofas and lone comfy seats are all in right places to keep guests comfortably seated while waiting for whatever takes them there. My meeting with a couple of people was just at a poolside section of the hotel. While not privileged to visit other facilities and floors of the stately multi-storey hotel complex, my overall impression of Lagos Marriot Hotel was that of an elegant, superlative 5-star hospitality built to global esteem.

author: Jite Ovueraye
review_text: Great service. Clean and well decorated. A pretty escape in the middle of ikeja.

author: Chikezie Nzewi
review_text: Great location. Staff were helpful and friendly. Check-in is after 3pm only, so plan accordingly. All the amenities you expect from a modern business/tourist hotel. Highly recommended.

author: Duke Nduka
review_text: This Marriott hotel by far is the cleanest and nicest Marriott hotel that I have stayed at period. The hotel is very clean with friendly staffs . The rooms are immaculate with all the  amenities you need. I will definitely stay here agin and again.

author: Ahmed Ademola Olugbile
review_text: A great hotel in GRA. It's ranking the number 1 patronised at the moment as it causes massive traffic on the road on a daily due to trooping (inflow and outflow) of different vehicles including government officials (noticeable with the siren and security presence). Security check before entry and underground parking. State of the art facilities within. Five star restaurant serving and great service.

author: Richard Chinze
review_text:
It was nice visiting this amazing hotel,  Marriott Ikeja. I had a swell time with awesome welcome by one of the staff by name Rilwan. Thank you Rilwan, because my next trip to Ikeja, you made me have reason to visit Marriott Ikeja again.

Really a place to be.

author: Olamide Akande
review_text:
I had an amazing time in Marriott Hotel in Ikeja, I didn't lodge though but the rooms are amazing. I had a dinner at the Restaurant, which is run by koriko and co.
The Restaurant is a buffet settings where you get various foods, fruits and pastries. You could total have a three course meal with the varieties. The environment is lovely, the staff that attended to us to was very nice and also it is pretty affordable. I forgot to mention that drinks are not part of the price of the buffet, in other words you pay for the drinks separately.

author: Ade Okuneye
review_text:
Mariott Ikeja is a beautiful venue with great architecture and construction execution, unfortunately the following need to be looked into urgently.

1.) A lot of the rooms have a damp/mouldy smell which is a health hazard. There needs to be a quick investigation on what is causing this. Most likely it's due to lack of ventilation in a hot humid environment. I requested for a different room due to the strong smell of mould in the 1st room but the second wasn't too far off. I think most rooms are like this and they need to be put right.

2.) I'm an advocate of Nigerian culture, the lunch that my family and I had today at Marriot has to be the most disgusting I've ever had. There seems to be great emphasis on food from other Nationalities than there is of Nigeria's.  If you go to China for instance, they don't sell you Eba with Efo riro, they seel you their entire food type options, so why are you watering down our Culture to gain certain audience. Be proud of our food and help other cultures learn how to consume it.

The Head Chef needs to be checked as the food was disgusting right down to common Suya and grilled Lamb. It needs to be put right please.

author: Devesh Sarwate
review_text:
Nice hotel close to the lagos airport which is good for business visits.

The restaurants have decent food but are mostly ridiculously expensive (Buffet at the local food restaurant is USD $50 per person)

The M lounge is one of the best features of this hotel and having access to it would be great. We had access to the lounge because of loyalty status and enjoyed it a lot.

The Indian food at the wakame restaurant deserves a special mention. The Indian food here is great, please try it.

The one complaint I had was that the shower water outlet was bad and water got accumulated there. Also the shower door did not stay closed spilling water outside. I hope they fix these things.

author: Elizabeth Amah
review_text:
An absolute pleasure for me and my children to had stayed at the Marriot Hotel Ikeja.
The majestic grandeur of the interior  exudes the wow factor the environment of the hotel and our room were also clean and serene.
Thanks to all the staff especially those at the reception most particularly Helen and Blessing your service was magnificent.💎🥇👍🏾🥰

author: Baribefe Vite
review_text: Our stay at Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja was simply outstanding; a fusion of luxury, excellent hospitality, and mouth-watering cuisine. The staff, including Maureen Nwankwo at the front desk, Bridget, Joy, and Helen, showed genuine care and exceeded our expectations with their attentiveness. Porters Kenneth and George ensured smooth transitions, while Peter's superb service at Azure restaurant elevated our dining experiences. This hotel masterfully delivered on all fronts, offering an unmatched stay in Lagos that truly felt like a home away from home. We can't wait to return!

author: adewunmi adetosoye
review_text: Great hotel experience.... very exquisite

author: Millennial Señora
review_text: My 3 nights staycation was awesome, though i had a few turn offs with a few staffs but it doesn't change my opinion about the other good stuffs. I'll give Mr Peter at Koriko&Co restaurant a 5 star as well. The restaurant (Koriko) can be very busy & occupied on weekends. The ambience, aesthetics, room service & coziness of the sheets were 👌. author: M S
review_text: I have earned platinum status with Marriott and it means absolutely nothing to this hotel!!  They do not give me access to the executive lounge and don't offer me a complimentry breakfast.  I was trapped in the elevator for approximately 5 minutes with no help in sight I rang the alarm to no avail.  Finally the elevator technician was able to open the door and let me out.  This hotel provides a tiny happy hour for approximately 100 flight crew from different airlines which runs out of food in about 5 minutes and is not replenished in a timely manner!!!  Nice hotel with lousy customer service!!  This is why I no longer spend money on Marriott properties!!!! Their excuse for not giving me my platinum benefits is the lousy happy hour with not enough food or drinks for the amount of people who attend!!!

author: glory isiorho
review_text: Horrible service and food, I wouldn't recommend eating here. Yesterday, I went with some gym members for suya and fries/fried yam. Firstly, we were seated at an extreme corner with appalling ventilation close to the poolside toilet and kitchen. Secondly, what was supposed to be chapman drink, looked like cool aid and tasted bland. Thirdly, the waitress was busy attending to multiple customers simultaneously so it took over an hour before our meal was ready. Finally, when the meal came, there was portion discrepancies, and the suya wasn't palatable.

author: Zi Ben-Aroh
review_text:
In the realm of culinary desires, my friend and I found ourselves lured by the irresistible temptation of a lavish buffet. Yet, the fates conspired against our gastronomic aspirations. Time, relentless in its December, left me entangled in a web of tasks, causing a delay that saw me arriving just as the lunchtime banquet concluded.

Undeterred by the closing window of feasting opportunity, my friend, driven by the formidable force of hunger, and I embarked on an alternative gastronomic journey. Seated icosycozy enclave, we transformed the setback into an unexpected opportunity for meaningful discourse.

As we sipped on the elixir of conversation, the hunger pangs faded into the background, eclipsed by the richness of our dialogue on matters ranging from the intricacies of business to the nuances of life itself. The ambient chatter and clinking of cutlery formed the soundtrack to our impromptu rendezvous, creating a setting where the convergence of appetite and intellect became a harmonious symphony.

In the end, what initially seemed like a thwarted quest for a buffet became a nourishing experience of connection and camaraderie, proving that sometimes, the most delectable moments are not found on a plate but in the company of a kindred spirit.

author: Seyi Oduntan OON
review_text:
A new, very modern hotel located at GRA Ikeja with lovely ambience, which has been tastefully furnished. The hotel has ample parking in its underground facility, manned by several personnel to avoid getting lost in its maze of confusing parking arrangement. The parking area was not well planned at all. But once parked, you are courteously guided to the security scanners and the lifts to the upper floors.

With the hype surrounding the hotel, I decided to try out the buffet restaurant on a Sunday and was very disappointed by the offerings.  The food pans were mostly empty when I arrived at 9.30am and the variety was limited when compared with the "Pumpkin Restaurant" at the Sheraton Hotel just down the road, which was also better priced. Within 2 minutes, I felt it wasn't worth it so I left for the Sheraton Hotel. I plan to lodge in one of the rooms in the near future for comparison with similar hotels in the same area.

author: Naija Walker
review_text:
So my 4-star rating reflects only my experience for the day. I do not make blanket statements that necessarily reflect their total service. So we went here to be part of the BOGOF promotions on the burgers organized by LostInLagos. So we had to use the semi-outdoors Azure Grills. I had issues with the cooling which didn’t work too well for me, I wish the inner seating spaces were cooler that day as it was scorching hot outside.

The burgers were quite nice, although limited variety and quite on the high side (the latter was not unexpected, I mean this is Marriott). Overall, the facility looks great and as they are still new in Lagos (less than 2 full months at the time of my visit), I believe they will keep iterating different things until they find their exact template.

author: bukola odubawo
review_text:
Currently staying at Marriot ikeja. This is actually my second stay but will be the last time that l will be staying at any Marriot Hotel based on the horrible experience that l encountered during my stay in Marriot lkeja .

I booked for 4 night in my first stay   then rebooked another 3night some days later but you know what? My 2nd stay experience was even worse than the 1st stay experience.

Issues: If you live in the UK and want a stress free vacation like me, please avoid Marriot Hotel lkeja because you will regret it.

BREAKFAST: Breakfast buffet is cold. I only eat pancake and bacon for breakfast but l couldn't eat this meal, so l ended up avoiding breakfast sometimes.

I was told l cannot sit in the outdoor garden beside the restaurant to have my breakfast but 2 separate male folks were allowed to eat in the outdoor garden. I can see them because it's a transparent door.

POOL: Do not bother going to the pool because the bar is just in front of the pool. Folks staring at you, staff staring at you and also harrasing you every minute telling you not to take photo of yourself.

Yes, l had more than 8 different staff approached me during my 1st stay telling me not to take a photo of myself in the hotel. I had to complain to the Manager to get this resolved.

White folks are given preferential treatment than black folks, yes l said that a 100%.

The most horrible aspect is that MALE FOLKS ARE GIVEN PREFERENTIAL TREATMENT THAN FEMALE FOLKS.

Sitting in the lounge with some other males and females who were also sitting down separately. The staff kept coming to harass me e.g: They be like: ma'am are you lost? Do you want anything? You can't sit here etc... I noticed some females were harassed as well. But the males were never approached or harassed.

I had to escalate to the manager before the harassment stopped.

Cleaning: I had to escalate to the manager after 2 episodes of my room not getting cleaned until nearly 4pm despite requesting for cleaning in the morning.

Staff at the wine bar by the poolside are so rude, unprofessional and uncouth. You are given attitude if ure a Female. Males are treated with so much respect. Females are treated like dirts... It is so appalling to experience this for a service that was paid for with my money.

The staff at the bar will argue with you at every opportunity and then ignore your table even when you request for them to take your order. They are rude, unfriendly and has no single courtesy for lodgers.

My hotel landline phone was not working, room was not cleaned despite my request, security was harrasing me over parking. I asked to speak with the manager, he apologised and promised to send a bottle of wine to my room as a consolation. Funny enough, l never got that wine, not like l cannot afford it, but never make promises of what you cannot fulfil. Speaking as an experienced manager myself.

The live band held today was horrible. I went downstairs, the place was filled up with visitors from outside.  A staff asked me and my sibling to sit in an empty table. Some other staff came and started harrasing me to get up that the table was reserved.

I had to involve the staff who told us to sit there to speak with this 2male staff that was so rude. She spoke to them and they left. But they ignored our table all through, l reported to another staff that we are still waiting for someone to take our orders. But they carried on to ignore us. A guy in charge came and spoke rudely. We ended up leaving the live band show in annoyance.
I ordered for dinner @11pm. 8 hour later, no food arrived. Yes, l phoned the reception, he said he will get back to me. He never did and l slept with an empty stomach.

In my whole years of travelling and staying in different places, Marriot Ikeja is my worse so far.

I usually prefer radissonblu on any vacation but changed my mind as my sister wanted me to stay at Marriot. Now l am left with a trauma from this experience at Marriot ikeja.

There is no free WiFi in the rooms....one has to pay to purchase data to browse the Internet. However, the conference rooms have free passworded Internet access.
There's a lobby bar, swimming pool, gym and club too.

author: Abi Balogun
review_text: Wow, wow, wow! I had a great time with my friends here when I visited Nigeria. The ambiance was excellent! We went to the Koriko and co buffet restaurant and the food was awesome! They had a nice variety of food, from the native jollof rice, to the gizzard pepper soup, to the Chinese fried rice, bread rolls, lots of desserts, meat, fish. The servers were very courteous! I remember Mr. Saviour because his name stood out and he was courteous. Chapman is a la carte. The buffet price was 13k naira when I visited in July 2021, with complimentary water and a can of soda pop.
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you so much for your kind words. We look forward to having you back at our hotel."}}

author: Muminat Adekunle
review_text:
Whatever you heard about Marriott Hotel, it's all that and more. I went there for a video shoot and the experience was awesome!

First, they are generally more liberal than most hotels allow you to be probably because they have underground locations within the hotel that are separate from actual lodges. So none of that, you can't make too much noise or go there noise like many upscale hotels make.

Whether it's a conference, meeting, wedding, shoot, Marriott ticks all the boxes in everything - magnificence, space, staff, coordination, gosh! I'm blown.

The bathrooms were so beautiful that I almost gave up the video shoot to stay there. I had a whole photoshoot in there😁
Lights in the bathroom are digitised such that they come on when you walk in.

There was a wedding holding there the day we went and from what I see, it's a choice location for that too. So if you are thinking about that,  just spend all your coins on Marriott, you won't regret it.

I did not visit the rooms at all but if the general service is anything to go back, it must be great too. Staff were literally waiting on people and going round to ask them for anything they can assist with.

Overall, tiri gbosa for Marriott. I'm super impressed.

author: joalicia hardy
review_text:
DO NOT STAY HERE IF YOU CAN BOOK SOMEWHERE ESLE WITH BETTER CUSTOMER SERVICE STANDARDS. I was a solo traveler, meeting my friend here and the hotel staff did not make me feel safe, more so, they made me feel unwanted and un heard. This is even more concerning in a country where we are told by everyone not to be alone at any time of the day. The customer service here is extremely poor. I arrived to checkin on time at 3pm, my room was not ready. I reserved a room with 2 beds because i was meeting my friend here for a wedding, and the receptionist insisted we take a king bed, because "the room is better" & "all of the double beds were booked" however, this is not what we chose when we made our reservations months in advance. My friend and I went to the spa, while we waited for our room to be ready, 4 hours later, and the room still was not ready for us!!! The management staff was not apologetic and quite RUDE during this major inconvenience they caused. I was in the lobby for at least an hour going back and forth with them. (how could they give our room away, and tell us they did, and not accommodate us for the inconvenience). We finally got the keys to our room at 10pm. If you can avoid staying here, please spend your money at a SAFER, better hotel with better service.   My friend and I ended up staying in a king room (that we did not request) until the next day. The hotel staff changed our room in the middle of our stay & didn't acknowledge my request to be compensated for one full day because of confusion on their part. I was visiting for a wedding & Im sure this establishment profited a large sum of money this weekend, from the wedding party alone.
We can not normalize this behavior. The staff here will ruin the Marriotts reputation if this continues.
owner_responses:
{"en": {"text": "Dear Joalica,
Thank you for choosing to stay at the Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja and providing your honest feedback.

Providing the highest level of hospitality is our number one priority and we sincerely apologize for falling short of meeting your expectations. As a teachable moment, we have shared your feedback with the appropriate hotel team to ensure the necessary guidelines are in place to prevent these issues from occurring in the future.

Once again, thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We appreciate you choosing to stay with us."}}

author: T O.O
review_text:
1. I requested early check-in a week before I arrived. They confirmed I would have early check-in. They did not honor this. I arrived after an 11 hour flight and they did not check me in until night time.

2. I was told I could use their spa room showers to freshen up and would not be disturbed. Twice a male and a female employee barged into the room while I was undressed.

3. Some products I purchased as gifts for family members was stolen by the hotel staff and they did nothing to try to resolve this issue nor were they concerned.

4. The shower pressure on the 5th floor failed the morning after my check-in so I was not able to shower before I left the hotel for my appointments.

The hotel lacks customer service and for the price is not worth it. Will not recommend this hotel to anyone I know going to Nigeria. The Radisson Blu was a better option.

author: Ahmad Barnoukh
review_text:
Decided to go and have dinner at wakame restaurant one of the most expensive restaurants at ikeja, I’ve ordered for Bao soft shell crab and butter chicken with coriander chips with side fried rice
My starter was very bad the crab was bitter and smelling, I asked the waiter to remove it noting that i just had one bite and he didn’t ask for the reason, he brought my main course  while the fried rice had shrimps inside it knowing that I just ordered for fried rice ( first I don’t eat shrimps because I’m an allergic against it and second I don’t know why I have to eat shrimps with my side fried rice and I’m eating it with the chicken) funny enough the waiter didn’t mention that it contains shrimps) I asked him to bring plain rice and I had to wait until they bring it to eat with my main course, well the rice was just boiled rice with poached egg and tasteless same as for the chicken so I immediately asked for the bill and left the food without eating any, the waiter didn’t ask for the reason even the chef was watching me since they have open kitchen and I was sitting in the bar, until I told him the food wasn’t nice he just said oh ok I will inform the chef, but unfortunately nobody came to take my complaint.. I wqs really disappointed and got to go another place to have my dinner
Be noted that I sent msg to their IG and they response  was ‘’thanks for your feedback’’ without asking for the details
Very bad experience, food quality was zero, customer service was zero

author: Lois Sankey
review_text:
Very beautifully done hotel.
But extremely poor front desk staff behaviour by one Happiness. She needs deep customer service training or be sent to the back office.
Very poor customer service generally. They did not respect our booking request for late checkout and they did not provide prior notice of any challenge.
They had a huge crowd of wedding parties and music award attendee yet they did not place ant security to manage the use of the lifts and ensure strick masks wearing at the lifts thus people were cramped into lifts without masks. I wore my masks religiously but fell ill within 3days and turns out to be covid19.  This I imagine the level of huge spread of infection that happened from this place between Friday to Monday of the week myself and my colleagues stayed. 2 of us got covid19 from the hotel and our colleague that visited us at the hotel also got infected.
Marriott needs to do better that they are doing for a newly opened hotel
Their chefs can also do better with the quality of meals they churn out reduce the maggi and focus more on really authentic taste
I will not be staying at this hotel again.

author: tope oyesanya
review_text:
If it were possible to give them a 10star I would
From the entrance to smooth valet parking, to the lobby, wide and  spacious, beautiful, relaxing I noticed there was no smell atall their air filter is very effective, you have options of where to sit
When we stepped into the dining area I couldn’t even smell food in the air just a pleasant light smell
The rest room was so on point that I was so comfortable making a video😂 I was expecting the usual smell of restrooms that I covered my nose before entering only to discover there was no bad smell
The food!!! Oh gosh everything tasted good
From the jollof rice , to the Russian salad, The pepper soup!!! Oh gosh the goat meat was sooooft and tasty. You know that kind of pepper that will clear your respiratory system? Yes that one
Then I tried the fisherman soup auuughhhh that was my reaction the eba was soft and hot, one morsel going down my throat was so relaxing and easy I could feel it going down like a sweet soft landing of an aircraft😂😂
I have never had chapman that does not water down after few minutes. This chapman was sweet to the end
I dint get to go up to the rooms but I am sure it will be on another level
I am definitely going back
It is great for both international and local visitors
It is a place to be!!!! 💯
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you so much for your kind words. We look forward to having you back at our hotel"}}

author: Rainer Meurer
review_text:
What is wrong at Lagos Marriott? And the answer is almost everything. We went to have dinner at the BBQ Pool area and it was simply put a disaster. Long waiting times of over an hour for the main course, a grilled meat with french fries that tastes of old oil. When asked what is happening, the kitchen is busy. Yes kitchen do get busy on Friday evening but the Exec Chef walking around making his tour and doing as everything is ok is just sad. Oh we had starters first those took almost as long.... So way over two hours in total, i mean starter's are complicated, Guacamole, Eggplant dip and fried Plantains (later the only dish that was decent to be very honest) why you have to reinvent Guacamole? It work for some 100s of years as it is, why but a whitish cream on it. The poor untrained waitress had overall no idea of the dishes, F&B Manager and Chef let the people try and explain to them what they are selling.... Please. Asking the waitress if the Guacamole comes with Tortilla chips, no, with what does it come? Just the cream aguacate, but how we eat it, answer with fork and knife... This dialog already says everything. Another taste? What kind of local beer you have, answer Guinness, Budweiser....
We where four people, the grill while passing by was mostly empty, food came out of the pool bar kitchen and it was not tasty, mit well prepared and taste of old oil.
When I ask something the Executive Chef he turned to the waiter or Service supervisor with the words, what he wants..... Thank you for you polite and exceptional service.
One of the Pilar's of Marriott Hotels has been always high hospitality and very well trained employees, exceptional well trained employees. Marriott Lagos destroys this legacy in one evening because of failure in the Management nothing else. Give your employees the tools to be able to make a good job. Create passion and be passionate by yourself, serving such a low quality food with such a low quality service was unexpected.
The environment is nice, the life music was very good.

author: Nosa E
review_text:
So I stayed here for 2 nights from Saturday to Monday celebrating with my wife to celebrate her birthday. Here is my review

Pros
- Ample parking space in their underground parking
- Loved the modern design of the room and the reception sitting area
- The lady who brought the birthday cake for my wife the next day could sang!
- The shower

Cons
- The guy who parked me asking for money IMMEDIATELY I stepped out of the car. The dude was like “good evening sir, welcome to Marriot. Anything for the boys?”. The security guy who scanned my box also did the samething
- Par boiled jollof rice at the grill area on Saturday night. Par boiled white rice for room service on Saturday night. Par boiled white rice at the Sunday morning buffet
- Shower cap packet was empty but left in our bathroom
- Huge con: Room service. Mentioned rice was par boiled. Extra salty stew. Food not covered (see pic)

Blah points
- The blah tasting cake they brought for my wife on her birthday
- The blah breakfast buffet. Some variety please

Overall, I was not impressed. The place is pricey and there was no WOW factor throughout our stay

author: JF BÜ
review_text:
I was here one months after opening, this was 9 months ago. In feb 2022. I had the impression It was an excellent hotel, new standard, well managed. Clean.

I now came back in November 2022 and I need to say I am shocked how you can run down a hotel in that short time.

It is overall dirty place, the lobby furniture is partly disgusting, the rooms are not cleaned (because people don’t know how to clean, and this is a manager issue, not the fault of the people they need to be thought that a vacuum cleaner has to be used every day, that the table needs to be washed after having made a party on it), many things are broken. The furniture is dirty and in partly bad conditions, things are not repaired but just accepted that it is run down.

This is a shame for a Mariott, I give it 6 more months and it will almost be same ugly condition as the Sheraton hotel in Ikeja

Mariott needs to do somethung here urgently

author: ICCG ICCG
review_text:
Edit: During my most recent stay I noticed the hotel showed moderate wear and tear. They hosted a number of  events during my stay and the building took a hit, I.e,  trash left in the elevator, stained carpets and inadequate rooms. There was a full blown photo shoot in the lobby with a groom and his noisy entourage. Furthermore, the GM is nonexistent as they never respond to guest complaints nor do they follow up on guests compliments. I hope that Marriott International takes note of this and works with management to get this once top notch hotel back to A status. Then and only then will my ratings increase. The front desk staff (including bellhops and security) is the best part of the hotel.

As a loyal Marriott fan, I’ve stayed in its top brands like J. W. Marriott, Ritz Carlton, etc. While it’s not a Ritz Carlton, the staff and the ambience sets up an experience that is close to it! Well organized check in process, visible security measures, clean rooms and nice amenities. Some of the in room fixtures could use some extra tlc but the other service areas make up for it. This is my family’s choice hotel when visiting Lagos.

author: Olukemi Ogunsakin
review_text:
Lagos Mariott Hotel is a beautiful hotel, aestheticically pleasing and stays true to the international Marriott standards. Its probably the most expensive hotel in Lagos mainland today and that's easily understandable. As the new kid on the club, and since Lagosians like to be "outside", the crowd was ginormous.

I had dinner here so I'm going to focus on thier buffet experience.  They have a decent variety of meals and I always say that's the point to a buffet! I was not impressed with the goat meat peppersoup because the meat used was definitely stale. That deprived me of that fresh goat meat taste. They made up for that with the Pork chops though, super tasty. The rice meals were edible and not fantastic,  but the cream pasta gave me life. I also loved the sweet potato buns (that's not what it's called, can't remember). There was a wide array of salads, I plated a bit of each as shown in the image. Couldn't get to desert, already outdit myself.
I'll go again, for lunch this time and come back to update this review.

There's a live band performing at the pool side every Friday, they seemed like an interesting band. I couldn't stay because there were no free seats. Lagos people like to groove too much!

review_text: Lobby restaurants have the worst service you can ever think of, food is good, drinks are good, rooms service is just as bad.

author: Ben Keshiro
review_text: In my opinion the Lagos Marriott Hotel, Ikeja, is currently the best hotel in Lagos. Conveniently located (10 mins from the airport), seamless check in, spotless room, very friendly and helpful professional staff. It has a variety of fabulous restaurants and a fully equipped spacious gym. The perfect ecosystem of work hub, leisure and relaxation. They've nailed it!  Well done.

author: Naomi
review_text:
The ambiance of the hotel is so welcoming. very beautiful waiting area with good lightening, Top notch decorAnd of course a good background for pictures.
The koriko & co restaurant is a buffet restaurant. The buffet covers the food and a bottle water, the drinks is not included. They dont have enough options for Nigeria dishes (coconut rice, white rice, plantain lasagna, pounded yam with either ogbono or edikaikong) but in general the food was tasty. Moving to the desert and fruit, just two major fruits was available watermelon and pineapple. The had different kind of cakes .
A lot of foreign delicacy and soup to try.....including beef steaks.
You have to scan QR code to get the menu for the drinks
The environment of the restaurant was cool and neat but a low background music or instrumental wont be a bad idea. Plates and cutleries were packed immediately you are done eating and replaced.
The staffs were polite, well mannered and neatly dressed.
A reservation maybe needed especially on a Sunday
Generally it is a classy and huge hotel

author: Abimbola O.
review_text:
Luxury, 5-star and exceptional service!

World-class Hotel strategically located within Ikeja GRA. Top notch service delivered and high level of professionalism by the staff.

The hotel also has the following: Grill, buffet restaurant, swimming pool and pool deck, bar, lounges, outdoor dining, Gym, SPA amongst others. It's also provides amazing and beautiful sapces for the gram.

Isade Spa delivers exceptional massage sessions. The service is top-notch and you actually feel you're getting more value for the money paid. You get complimentary drink, coffeeand tea in addition. You also get to use the sauna and steam room after the massage session. There's also a lovely ambience for beautiful pictures.

Absolute value for every naira spent.

author: arnold eric
review_text:
I checked into this property on the 20th June evening 2021, the guy at the front desk In the name of Bayo, gave me a standard room rate for N159 thousand a night, precisely room 410, and the room was damped and smelling. Was like a celebrated chalets in Brazil. Tskbg shower in the cubicle, water was Escaping the glass door of the shower cubicle and it was a poor workmanship and inferior finishing.  I regret paying such amount for a night in a hotel that has many front desk staff with vested interest at the expense of guests. The people at the front desk could not give me receipt for the rate I paid, and  for hours they where giving silly excuses of system crash and internet connection problems.
.....They were using their brains  to manipulate guests and draw monies from guest which the management are not aware. That was the worst experience I have ever had in a hotel that claimed to be iconic but the reverse is the case here...The telephone the room I stayed  overnight was out of service. Horrible experience...... I will void this property and won’t advise anyone looking for luxury and comfort to go there. In the days ahead, I’ll write more on my experience in Ikeja Marriott hotel at Joel Ogunike.
I was so disappointed and I’m withdrawing my loyalty with Marriott hostel.

author: kakam nwosu
review_text: Marriot is a worldwide brand. De Ikeja brand lives de part. No doubt abt dat. Located in a posh part of Ikeja - Lagos, it definitely lives up to its brand. While driving past de front, it looks like any other average but well branded hotel. But step inside & explore! Let's not talk abt de usual; like de mighty well crafted reception hall etc.  De underground parking area is something else. Voluminous & well structured. Also, de back side of de hotel is evidently huge & well arranged or should i say organised. Crystal clear swimming pool with outdoor dining & drinking areas close by that is large enough for live bands to entertain revellers. De cuisine on offer is extensive,  both local, continental & international. De rooms are crisp clean, cousy & homely wit top-notch finishing. There is even an art gallery for collectors or admirers to view. This hotel is a marvel to behold & explore. Hhhmmmmmm.....however, pls note dat it isn't for de shallow pockets. U know wat I mean?

review_text: A truly Marriott in all ramifications. Beautiful setting, close to the airport and other amenities like shopping mall and restaurants. However, there is need for the hotel to control crowd, I noticed (and mentioned to one of the managers, people who are not lodgers have access to virtually every areas of the hotel. Also, the lobby was too crowded with non lodgers. The entire lobby needs rearrangement, customers have to walk through crowded lounge to the elevator, on few occasions, people with access keys jumped into the elevator with me to catch a ride upstairs. author: Tochukwu Uzoho
review_text:
A five star luxury hotel under the Marriot chain, located on Joel Ogunnaike street Ikeja.
You are welcomed at the gates by courteous security personnel before driving up the entrance. The entire environment oozes luxury with an unmatched ambience.
This place boasts of 3 elegant restaurants: the Azure Terrace, Wakame Spice and the Korico & Co, each offering its set of unique and tasty experience.
There is also the Isade spa and a well equipped gym to enable you carry on with your fitness journey while you are there.
The location is about 10mins drive to the local airports and about 15mins to the Murtala Muhammed international airport.
If you are looking for a taste of class and luxury, then this is the place.

author: lolia K
review_text:
Unfortunately I was not able to experience this hotel in person as I actually didn't stay. I had a series of Unfortunate events including some personal health issues that prevented me from me being able to stay in the hotel on said day.

I reached out to booking.com, but after giving me the run around, they eventually told me that only the hotel at this point could do anything about it as the booking day had passed. They mentioned they sent an email to the hotel and to wait for a response, nothing! Even tried reaching out again, still nothing.

Still unwell, I sent someone to the Marriot in ikeja to plead my case, Feeling absolutely no emphaty, they categorically said that there's nothing they could do. Deflated as the Marriot in Nigeria is SUPER expensive I went there personally to see if at least, they would give me the opportunity to stay the night at ANY OTHER time that was convenient but, nothing!

Was told
1. They NEVER received any email from Booking.com. which really got me upset because now it means someone lied to me (either booking.com or the Marriot).
2. They spoke about their policy which ultimately turned into a NO, also stating that booking.com had gotten their 30% so, nothing.

Even AIRLINES (Notorious for rejections) since the onset of the pandemic have been more compassionate about rescheduling with customers who come to plead their case for any reason.

At this point, not sure who to be more disheartened by...
Marriot who just started their business whose goal should be customer satisfaction or
Booking.com, years of use, who showed that they do not care at all about the client's resolution as long as their money is complete.

$330 ultimately means nothing to these 2 establishments, but means a WHOLE LOT to me a middle class client.

...........
UPDATE

I was eventually reached out to by a Senior leadership team member.

Not only was he gracious and apologised on behalf of his team, he ensured I got my full refund and also offered me a night's stay in the Hotel.

Thank you!
owner_responses:
{"en": {"text": "Dear Lolia,

Thank you for choosing the Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja and providing your honest feedback on the Guest Satisfaction Survey. Providing the highest level of hospitality is our number one priority and we sincerely apologize for falling short of meeting your expectations.

As a teachable moment, we have shared your feedback with the appropriate hotel team to ensure the necessary guidelines are in place to prevent this issue from occurring in the future. Once again, thank you for taking the time to share your feedback.

Regards"}}

author: Mayowa Muyide
review_text:
Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja is Located in Lagos, 3.2 km from Kalakuta Museum, Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja provides accommodations with an outdoor swimming pool, free private parking, a terrace and a restaurant. Providing a bar, the property is located within 11.6 km of National Stadium Lagos. The property has a 24-hour front desk, airport transportation, room service and free WiFi throughout the property.

The units are equipped with air conditioning, a flat-screen TV with satellite channels, a minibar, a electric tea pot, a shower, free toiletries and a desk. All rooms come with a private bathroom with a hairdryer, while selected rooms here will provide you with a balcony and others also have city views. At the hotel rooms are equipped with bed linen and towels.

A buffet, à la carte or continental breakfast is served at the property.

National Art Theatre is 14.5 km from Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja, while Synagogue Church Of all Nations is 17.7 km away. The nearest airport is Murtala Muhammed International Airport, 3.1 km from the accommodation.
Everything about Marriott Hotel is so Amazing and Tremendous, every time i come to this Beautiful Haven, is like am in the Western World.

author: Moshud
review_text: A very pleasant place to stay. Lagos Marriott, Ikeja is comparable to any 5* hotel in the world. The luxuries were well designed and purposeful.  Interior decoration was local. Staff were well trained and helpful. Three choice of restaurants, I specifically liked koriko and buffet menu. Their food was rich and well prepared. author: Segun Adeniji
review_text:
As far as staycations go, Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja did not disappoint during my family’s recent maiden stay at the property! Our journey began with Bayo at the Front Desk whose professionalism was as impressive as Front Desk Supervisor Elizabeth’s efficiency in ensuring we wanted for nothing.

Henry, the amiable Concierge gave us a tour of the hotel’s range of tastefully decorated rooms and it is easy to understand why LMHI consistently enjoys high occupancy rates. The naturally accommodating and thoughtful Chike Ogeah, Managing Director of the hotel owner’s hospitality subsidiary, went over and beyond to ensure my wife, our daughter and I had a memorable overall experience.

The hotel’s well-equipped gym and swimming pool were put to frequent use after indulging (or more accurately in my case, over-indulging!) in the property’s restaurants including the poolside grill. My wife and I were pampered at the spa during our couple’s massage by therapists Vera and Faith…hopefully I’ve banked a few extra brownie points with my wife! :)

The spacious underground car park is a great feature as it provides a welcome respite from the brutal Lagos sun. I suggest that a concave mirror is installed by the car park exit to avoid accidents between vehicles leaving the car park and those coming down the ramp from the front of the hotel.

Although described as a business hotel I unreservedly recommend Lagos Marriott Hotel Ikeja for all manner of guest and traveler. That said, the hotel’s meeting rooms, one of which we saw after it was set up for an international conference, and ballroom are first-class. Although a big political convention was taking place at the hotel during the first two days of our stay, service standards remained high throughout. God willing, my family and I will return soon to continue from where we left off..!
owner_responses: {"en": {"text": "Thank you so much for your kind words. We are glad you enjoyed your stay with us. We look forward to having you back at our hotel."}}
